metadata.title: Arbitration & ADR — the private/quasi-judicial dispute-resolution layer: US providers, forced-arbitration political economy, international commercial + investor-state (ISDS), per-state/religious/tribal, and on-chain (documented vs contested, dated)
metadata.status: Institutional facts (governance, scale, caseloads), the FAA->SCOTUS line, the National Arbitration Forum consent judgment, mass arbitration, the CFPB-rule repeal, EFAA, the ICSID/ISDS framework, the Energy Charter Treaty exodus, and the on-chain protocols' mechanics are individually sourced FACT. 'Industry bias as intent', 'whose access-to-justice numbers win', and 'on-chain manipulation intent' are CONTESTED inferences, flagged. Composition guard throughout: institutions are described by documented rules/outcomes, not treated as a single mind.
metadata.frame: Where disputes actually get resolved OUTSIDE the regular courts. Six layers: (1) national US private providers (AAA/ICDR, JAMS, FINRA Dispute Resolution Services, the defunct National Arbitration Forum); (2) federal bodies (FMCS, National Mediation Board); (3) the FORCED-arbitration political economy — the Federal Arbitration Act (1925) expanded by SCOTUS (Concepcion 2011, Amex v. Italian Colors 2013, Epic Systems 2018, Viking River 2022) to enforce consumer/employment class-action WAIVERS, the mass-arbitration counter-move (Keller Postman), the CFPB arbitration-rule repeal (2017), and the 2022 sexual-assault carve-out (EFAA); (4) international commercial (ICC/LCIA/SIAC/HKIAC/CIETAC) + investor-state ISDS (ICSID under the World Bank; PCA; UNCITRAL WG III reform; ECT exodus) enforced by the New York Convention; (5) per-state court-annexed/agency/religious/tribal ADR; (6) on-chain (Kleros, Aragon Court, UMA). Cross-links FINRA, the mass-tort blocks (class-waivers), World_Bank/IMF (ISDS), and the crypto/prediction-market blocks.

layer1_us_providers: AAA (American Arbitration Association) — 501(c)(3) nonprofit (1926; CEO Bridget McCormack, ex-Michigan CJ), its ICDR handling international cases; ~$29B in 2025 B2B claims, and now dominated by MASS ARBITRATION (~280,000 individual filings across 92 mass cases in 2024); consumer fee-shifting caps the consumer at ~$200. JAMS — for-profit but NEUTRAL-OWNED (~1/3 of neutrals hold equal shares; NOT a recent PE buyout — that premise is FALSE, a same-name confusion); ex-judge-heavy; ~22,000 filings/2025; Monster Energy v. City Beverages (9th Cir. 2019) vacated an award for an arbitrator/co-owner's non-disclosure (appearance standard, not proven actual bias). FINRA Dispute Resolution Services — the near-mandatory securities forum (FINRA is an SEC-overseen SRO, not a govt agency); Rule 2268 pre-dispute clause; public/non-public arbitrator classification; the EXPUNGEMENT pipeline (broker-record removal) reformed by the SEC-approved Special Arbitrator Roster (eff. Oct 16 2023) after PIABA found ~90%+ grant rates on unopposed requests. National Arbitration Forum (NAF) — the 2009 SCANDAL: MN AG Lori Swanson sued alleging NAF concealed debt-collection-industry ties (Accretive LLC/Axiant/Mann Bracken); consent judgment (~Jul 20 2009) exited it from consumer arbitration (no admission of liability). grade: institutional facts FACT; 'designed to defraud'/'rigged' inferences CONTESTED.
layer2_federal_bodies: FMCS (Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service) — independent agency (Taft-Hartley 1947) mediating labor disputes; targeted by EO 14238 (Mar 14 2025) for reduction 'to the minimum required by law' (~95% staff cut reported), then a district court PERMANENT injunction/summary judgment (Rhode Island v. Trump, Nov 21 2025; govt appeal pending) on APA/separation-of-powers grounds. National Mediation Board — independent agency (Railway Labor Act) for rail/air labor (mediation + representation elections). Federal court-annexed arbitration runs under the ADR Act of 1998 (consent-based, non-binding, capped). grade: fact.
layer3_forced_arbitration: The core power story. The Federal Arbitration Act (1925, 9 U.S.C. sec.2) made written arbitration agreements enforceable; its expansion to consumer/employment ADHESION contracts was JUDICIAL: Southland (1984, FAA preempts state law) -> Gilmer (1991, statutory employment claims arbitrable) -> Rent-A-Center (2010, delegation clauses) -> AT&T Mobility v. Concepcion (2011, 5-4, class-action WAIVERS enforceable) -> American Express v. Italian Colors (2013, enforceable even if individual cost exceeds recovery) -> Epic Systems v. Lewis (2018, employment class-waivers, NLRA doesn't override FAA) -> Viking River v. Moriana (2022, partial PAGA preemption). Effect: forced arbitration + class-waivers pulled consumer/employment disputes OUT of courts and class actions (Wells Fargo fake-accounts, nursing homes, gig misclassification). COUNTER-MOVE = MASS ARBITRATION: plaintiff firms (Keller Lenkner->Keller Postman) file tens of thousands of individual demands so the business's own per-case AAA/JAMS fees become the leverage (Uber ~$18M fees 2019; DoorDash Abernathy 5,010 drivers + ~$9.5M, Judge Alsup 2020; Amazon removed its arbitration clause after ~75,000 Alexa demands, 2021). AAA/JAMS issued 2024 mass-arbitration fee rules; the 7th Cir. (Wallrich v. Samsung, Jul 2024) checked forced fee-payment. CFPB arbitration rule (banning financial class-waivers, issued Jul 2017) was REPEALED via the CRA (Senate 51-50, VP Pence; signed Nov 1 2017). EFAA (Ending Forced Arbitration of Sexual Assault/Harassment Act, signed Mar 3 2022) is the one durable rollback; the broader FAIR Act has passed the House but never the Senate (reintroduced 2025, not enacted). grade: the doctrine/statutes/votes are FACT; whether the majority is 'dismantling access to justice' (dissents' framing) is CONTESTED; the Chamber/Institute-for-Legal-Reform 'consumers win more in arbitration' vs the ~10,400-to-1 non-filing rate (Colvin/EPI) is a normative dispute over WHOSE experience counts.
layer4_international: Enforced globally by the New York Convention (1958, 172 states) + the UNCITRAL Model Law. Commercial institutions: ICC International Court of Arbitration (Paris; ~894 new cases 2025; distinctive Art.34 award scrutiny), LCIA (London; hourly-fee model), SIAC (Singapore; ~886 cases 2025, record ~$14.5B), HKIAC (Hong Kong; ~388/2025; post-2020 National Security Law neutrality concerns [CONTESTED inference] vs record caseloads), CIETAC (China; CCPIT-embedded [structural link FACT; 'state-directed bias' CONTESTED]). INVESTOR-STATE (ISDS): ICSID (World Bank Group; 1965 Convention; 1,058 cumulative cases by mid-2025) + UNCITRAL/SCC/PCA-administered claims let corporations sue sovereign states under BITs/FTAs (~1,401 known treaty cases by end-2024 per UNCTAD). Legitimacy critique: 'regulatory chill', secret ad hoc tribunals, repeat-player arbitrators, asymmetry (only investors sue); defense: depoliticizes disputes, states win a large share (~1/3 investor / ~1/3 state / balance settled). UNCITRAL Working Group III adopted an arbitrator Code of Conduct (Jul 2023) and is negotiating a standing multilateral investment court. Energy Charter Treaty EXODUS: France/Germany/Poland/UK and the EU itself withdrew (EU effective Jun 28 2025) over ISDS/climate — but the 20-year sunset clause still protects existing investments. PCA (Permanent Court of Arbitration, The Hague; Yukos ~$50B; South China Sea award 2016, rejected by China). CAS (Court of Arbitration for Sport, Lausanne): Semenya v. Switzerland (ECtHR Grand Chamber, Jul 10 2025) raised the judicial-review standard over sports arbitration. grade: institutional facts FACT; bias/neutrality inferences CONTESTED.
layer5_state_religious_tribal: Per-state/sub-national: court-annexed NON-BINDING arbitration for smaller civil cases (PA 42 Pa.C.S. sec.7361 cap $50k; CA judicial arbitration); state-bar mandatory fee arbitration (CA MFAA sec.6200-6206; NY Part 137); state-agency adjudication (workers' comp boards, PUC complaint processes, ACA insurance external review by IROs, 'lemon law' arbitration incl. BBB AUTO LINE). Uniform Arbitration Act (1955, ~35 states) / Revised UAA (2000, ~22-23 states + DC — exact count uncertain). Religious/private (binding only via a signed FAA-enforceable agreement): rabbinical courts (Beth Din of America; three-judge din torah; also administers get/Jewish divorce via halachic prenup); Christian conciliation (Rules of Procedure for Christian Conciliation; NYT 'Scripture Is the Rule of Law', Nov 2 2015 vs religious-liberty/freedom-of-contract defenders). TRIBAL COURTS are a DISTINCT SOVEREIGN judiciary (NOT arbitration; power doesn't depend on an agreement) — Oliphant (1978)/Montana (1981) frame jurisdiction; the 'rent-a-tribe' payday-lending abuse (tribes paid ~1-2% to lend their sovereign immunity + tribal-arbitration clauses to dodge usury caps) was largely REJECTED by courts (Jackson v. Payday Financial 7th Cir. 2014 'sham and illusion'; Hayes v. Delbert 4th Cir. 2016; CFPB v. CashCall, cert denied Mar 2 2026). grade: fact (RUAA count and lemon-law specifics flagged).
layer6_onchain: On-chain 'arbitration' — Schelling-point crowd-juror protocols. Kleros (Ethereum; PNK-staking jurors, commit-reveal, appeals; ~$7M token mcap) — used for escrow/Proof-of-Humanity/token registries; critiqued as plutocratic/bribery-exposed (Kleros concedes + lists defenses); the 2022 Proof-of-Humanity split ended in a mutually-authored fork, not a hostile takeover. Aragon Court (ANJ/ANT jurors) — very low traction; the Aragon Association canceled its ~$200M treasury-to-DAO transfer (May 9 2023) amid an activist-investor fight ('51% attack' per Aragon vs 'legitimate governance' per Arca [CONTESTED intent]) and DISSOLVED (Nov 2 2023); Court/Protocol is legacy (repo archived 2025). UMA Optimistic Oracle — token-holder DVM votes; the resolution layer for POLYMARKET prediction markets; contested resolutions (Ukraine-mineral market ~$7M Mar 2025; Zelenskyy-'suit' market ~$160M+ Jun-Jul 2025 [whale-rigging alleged, unproven, genuinely ambiguous question]; Barron/$DJT market Jun 2024 which Polymarket publicly said UMA 'got wrong'); structural conflict — voters can bet on the market they resolve; base rate <2% of assertions disputed. ENFORCEABILITY (fact): on-chain awards self-execute for crypto but generally FAIL the New York Convention off-chain (no seat, pseudonymous jurors, due-process) unless re-anchored in a conventional seated framework (the hybrid path; a Kleros ruling recognized by a Mexican court ~2021). grade: mechanics FACT; 'coordinated manipulation intent' CONTESTED/unproven.
the_honest_reading: Arbitration is a vast privatized-justice layer whose backbone is settled law (FAA -> Concepcion/Epic/Italian Colors/Viking River) that moved most US consumer and employment disputes out of courts and class actions via class-action WAIVERS. The clearest documented abuse was forum-specific (the 2009 National Arbitration Forum consent judgment over concealed creditor ties), and the clearest documented counter-force is mass arbitration (DoorDash/Abernathy; Amazon dropping its clause). The FINRA securities forum, the ICSID/ISDS investor-state regime (World Bank-housed, backlash-driven ECT exodus), and the on-chain protocols (Kleros/Aragon/UMA) are the same private-adjudication logic at different scales. Read by the composition guard: the repeat-player statistics (Colvin), the FINRA arbitrator-pool skew (Egan/Matvos/Seru), CIETAC's state linkage, and the crypto whale-vote cases are documented STRUCTURES and STATISTICS — not proof of a unified biased 'mind'; those inferences are graded contested. Honesty flags carried: JAMS is neutral-owned (the 'recent PE buyout' premise is false); 'ITAB' as a mass-arb firm is unverified; the exact RUAA state count and current ISDS win-split are approximate. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: Minnesota AG — lawsuit v. National Arbitration Forum (2009)
N.D. Cal. — Abernathy v. DoorDash (Alsup, 5,010 drivers, 2020)
US Chamber Institute for Legal Reform — 'Mass Arbitration Shakedown' (Feb 2023)
EPI/Colvin — mandatory arbitration covers ~60M workers; repeat-player effect
UNCTAD — 1,401 known ISDS cases by end-2024
EU Council — Energy Charter Treaty withdrawal (effective Jun 28 2025)
White & Case — Semenya v. Switzerland (ECtHR Grand Chamber, Jul 10 2025)
4th Cir. — Hayes v. Delbert (rent-a-tribe arbitration voided, 2016)
NYT — 'In Religious Arbitration, Scripture Is the Rule of Law' (Nov 2 2015)

edges.raw_instrument: largest US ADR provider (AAA/ICDR); ~$29B 2025 B2B claims; ~280,000 mass-arbitration filings in 2024; consumer fee-shifting
for-profit but NEUTRAL-OWNED ex-judge-heavy provider (~22,000 filings/2025); Monster Energy v. City Beverages 2019 disclosure vacatur
edges.note: OVERLAY: L1 (the 'recent PE buyout' premise is FALSE)

edges.raw_instrument: the near-mandatory securities forum (Rule 2268 clause; public/non-public arbitrators; broker-record EXPUNGEMENT pipeline reformed Oct 2023)
Dispute Resolution Services is FINRA's arbitration/mediation arm (FINRA = SEC-overseen SRO)
self-regulatory organization under SEC oversight; runs the securities-arbitration forum + BrokerCheck/CRD
2009 MN AG suit alleging concealed debt-collection ties (Accretive/Axiant/Mann Bracken); consent judgment exited it from consumer arbitration (no admission)
Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service (Taft-Hartley 1947); EO 14238 gutting (Mar 2025) enjoined (Rhode Island v. Trump, Nov 2025)
independent agency (Railway Labor Act) for rail/air labor mediation + representation elections
FAA 1925 (9 U.S.C. sec.2) — the statute SCOTUS expanded (Southland/Concepcion/Italian Colors/Epic/Viking River) to enforce consumer/employment class-action WAIVERS
plaintiff counter-move: tens of thousands of individual demands turn the business's own per-case fees into leverage (Uber 2019; DoorDash Abernathy 2020; Amazon dropped its clause 2021)
Keller Lenkner->Keller Postman pioneered the mass-arbitration model (the 'ITAB' firm is UNVERIFIED)
removed its consumer arbitration clause in 2021 after ~75,000 Alexa mass-arbitration demands (action documented; motive inferred)
US Chamber of Commerce's Institute for Legal Reform — leads pro-arbitration advocacy ('Mass Arbitration Shakedown'; 'consumers win more in arbitration' study)
2017 rule banning financial class-waivers (backed by CFPB's 2015 study) REPEALED via the CRA (Senate 51-50 VP Pence; signed Nov 1 2017); CFPB v. CashCall on rent-a-tribe
ICC International Court of Arbitration (Paris) — leading commercial institution (~894 cases 2025); Art.34 award scrutiny
London Court of International Arbitration (~362/2024; hourly-fee model; 100+ Russia-sanctions arbitrations post-2022)
Singapore International Arbitration Centre (~886 cases 2025, record ~$14.5B); state-aligned-but-independent
Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (~388/2025); China bridge (2019 Interim Measures); post-NSL neutrality concerns (contested) vs record caseloads
China's principal foreign-related institution; CCPIT/CCOIC-embedded (structural link FACT; 'state-directed bias' contested); 2012 SHIAC/SCIA split
1958 treaty (172 states) making foreign arbitral awards portable/enforceable — the backbone that lets arbitration substitute for cross-border litigation
UN Commission on Int'l Trade Law: Model Law + Arbitration Rules; Working Group III ISDS reform (arbitrator Code of Conduct Jul 2023; standing investment court under negotiation)
Investor-State Dispute Settlement: corporations sue sovereign states under BITs/FTAs (~1,401 known cases end-2024, UNCTAD); 'regulatory chill' critique vs 'depoliticization/states win ~1/3' defense
ICSID is part of the World Bank Group (1965 ICSID/Washington Convention) — the dominant ISDS forum (1,058 cumulative cases by mid-2025)
World Bank ISDS centre; ad hoc tribunals + annulment (not appeal); the core investor-state forum
multilateral energy-investment treaty; ISDS/climate backlash -> EU + France/Germany/Poland/UK withdrawal (EU effective Jun 28 2025); 20-yr sunset clause persists
Permanent Court of Arbitration (The Hague; 1899/1907) — inter-state/investor-state/treaty arbitration (Yukos ~$50B; South China Sea 2016, rejected by China)
Court of Arbitration for Sport (Lausanne) — final sports forum; Semenya v. Switzerland (ECtHR Grand Chamber, Jul 10 2025) raised the judicial-review standard
per-state: court-annexed non-binding arbitration (PA/CA), bar fee-arbitration (CA MFAA, NY Part 137), agency adjudication (workers' comp, PUC, ACA external review, lemon-law/BBB AUTO LINE); UAA/RUAA

religious/private arbitration (Beth Din of America; Christian conciliation) — binding only via signed FAA-enforceable agreements; NYT 'Scripture Is the Rule of Law' (Nov 2015) vs freedom-of-contract defense
edges.note: OVERLAY: L5 religious (tribal courts are a distinct SOVEREIGN system, not arbitration)

edges.raw_instrument: on-chain Schelling-point crowd-juror protocol (PNK staking); escrow/Proof-of-Humanity; plutocracy/bribery critique; a ruling recognized by a Mexican court ~2021 (hybrid enforceability)
DAO dispute oracle (ANJ/ANT jurors); low traction; Aragon Association canceled its ~$200M treasury transfer (May 2023) and dissolved (Nov 2023); now legacy

UMA Optimistic Oracle / DVM token-vote — the resolution layer for Polymarket; contested resolutions (Zelenskyy-suit ~$160M Jul 2025; Barron/$DJT Jun 2024); voter-can-bet conflict; <2% of assertions disputed
